source: Original — Mörk Borg Module Runner scenario generator. Pairs with the canonical Adventure Seed tables in tables-gm.json.
site_types: A desecrated wayside chapel
A flooded crypt beneath a dead chapel
A burned farmstead, still smoking
A fog-drowned crossroads
A collapsed silver mine
A heretic's hidden warren
A frozen shrine to a forgotten saint
A gallows-hill with too many crows
A plague-house nailed shut from the outside
A toppled watchtower full of nests
A stone circle with the centre stone missing
An abandoned tax-collector's cottage with the door barred from inside
A dry riverbed that should not be dry
A bell-tower whose bell has been removed and buried
A roadside shrine to a saint nobody can name
A waystation overrun by something that left in a hurry
An apothecary's wagon, abandoned with the horse still hitched, bones-only
A field of grave-flags, none of which carry a name
A bone-pit cut into the hillside, with one fresh body on top
A drowned ferry with its rope still tied at both ends
A pilgrim's hostel where everyone is asleep at midday
A copse of trees that lean toward the same nothing
A blind miller's mill, wheel turning in still water
A confessional out in a field, with the priest's stole still inside
A merchant caravan stopped mid-meal, untouched
A monk's hermitage carved into a hillside that wasn't there last year
A sun-bleached battlefield with the dead still upright
site_atmospheres: Everything here is wet, and none of it is water.
The silence has weight, like a held breath.
Something has been waiting here longer than the stones have stood.
Cold light, no source. The shadows lean the wrong way.
The air tastes of old iron and new rot.
Birds will not land here. They know something.
The wind here speaks in old languages, badly.
The ground gives slightly underfoot, like it isn't quite ground.
Every step you take, a step is taken somewhere else, in answer.
The light is the colour of an unhealing wound.
The smell is sweetness and rot, in roughly equal parts.
The temperature dropped when you arrived. It has not come back up.
Whatever lived here left in a hurry. Whatever moved in took its time.
It feels watched from below.
The stones are warm where they have no right to be.
An old prayer is folded into the air, and refuses to unfold.
Mid-morning, but the light has the quality of just before a storm.
The space sounds smaller than it looks. Listen.
A faint scratching, in the walls, in the floor, in your ears.
Something is breathing here that is not you.
twists: The one who hired you wanted you dead, not successful.
The relic you came for is the thing that is killing this place.
The dead here are not the enemy — they are a warning.
Your patron and the villain are the same person.
Completing the task is exactly what the apocalypse needs.
There is no treasure. There was never any treasure.
The villain you came for died days ago. Something else is wearing them.
The relic was never lost — it was hidden, on purpose, for someone like you.
The party member with the cleanest hands is the one the place has been waiting for.
The objective you were told and the objective that actually matters are not the same.
The threat you came to end is the only thing keeping a worse threat asleep.
Half of this place is dreaming. The other half is what the dream remembers.
Someone in your patron's house is here, watching, and they are not on your side.
The local 'victim' is the architect. They have been counting on rescuers.
The Calendar of Nechrubel has a verse for this exact moment. You are in it.
What you do next is what the cult has been praying for. They know your name.
The relic must be destroyed where it was made. You can't take it home.
Bringing the villain to justice fulfils the prophecy. Killing them prevents nothing.
objectives: find the missing before the third bell
carry the truth back out alive
close the door that was opened
stop the rite before it finishes
bury what was never properly buried
pay a debt that someone else incurred
copy a verse that was never meant to be copied
silence a witness who saw the wrong thing
restore a song that was deliberately forgotten
light a fire in a place that has been told never to burn
finish a confession that was interrupted
destroy a record before the next reading
speak a name aloud where it can be heard, exactly once
climax_types: the final chamber that remembers everyone who entered
a throne of debt and bone
a flooded vault with one way out
a pit that has been waiting to be fed
a circle of chairs, all occupied, all watching
a sunken bath that has not been clean in a long time
a chamber whose ceiling is the underside of a graveyard
a hall of statues that were not statues
a sermon-pit lit by the wrong kind of fire
a single locked room nobody alive has the key to
a mid-air bridge of bone over a smell
a marble hall sealed with one door, opened from inside
a stage with chairs facing it, all occupied by the dead
hook_framing: finds you with no one else left to ask.
offers coin you both know is cursed.
does not ask so much as inform you.
is already dead when the message arrives, but the message remains.
catches up to you on the road and refuses to look you in the eye.
arrives in your dreams three nights in a row, with the same instructions.
has the work written on a strip of skin you don't remember signing.
offers you a debt forgiven that you didn't know you owed.
speaks in the voice of someone you used to know, and isn't them.
arrives by way of a bird that should not be able to speak.
pays in coin so old the kingdom that minted it has been forgotten.
comes with a name that opens doors and a face that closes them.
side_site_types: A locked chapel-vault on a side road
A surveyor's abandoned tent, charts still pinned
A hermit's stilt-house over black water
A rust-eaten reliquary in the brush
A bone-pit somebody filled in too quickly
A cracked obelisk nobody can read
A burnt shepherd's hut with a fresh footprint
A toll-booth without a road
A confessor's tent, abandoned mid-rite
A barn full of grain that hums when touched
A waterlogged carriage half-sunk in mud
A child's playhouse built on top of an older one, built on top of an older one
A clearing with a single chair facing nothing
A widow's cottage with three locks on the inside
A bone-collector's wagon parked too neatly
A tax-collector's lockbox, untouched in the road
A pilgrim's bell stand with the bell rope cut
A burnt heretic's pyre still warm
A grave that has been refilled and reopened, repeatedly
A bookseller's tent with one book on the table and no bookseller
side_secrets: A scroll bears a list of names — one of yours is on it.
A bone-key carved with the same mark as a door you have not yet seen.
A coin minted by a kingdom that no longer exists; warm to the touch.
A child's drawing of the villain, drawn from life, recent.
A ledger of debts owed to the climax-warden, marked PAID IN MEAT.
A jar of teeth labelled in someone's careful hand: 'mine.'
A bone with two teeth-marks, both fresh, neither matching any animal you know.
A note folded so many times it has shed its own corners.
A locket holding a hair that is still growing.
A pouch of seeds that are warm to the touch.
A confession written in a hand that resembles one of yours.
A flask of clear water that pours backward when tilted.
A coin pressed flat by something that wasn't a hammer.
A ledger of names with one struck through. The pen-line is wet.
A child's tooth on a string. The string is too long.
A cracked locket with a portrait the artist hadn't met yet.
A page from the Calendar of Nechrubel, dated tomorrow.
A handful of bone-meal in a soldier's hand, fingers still curled.
climax_keys: the saint's finger-bone, scrimshawed with a sigil
the heretic's mask of stitched skin
the broken seal of the dead chapel
an unsigned writ of passage in old ink
the bone whistle that no living throat can sound
a tooth pulled with the right tongs at the right hour
a phrase spoken backward in the right voice
a child's true name, written in their own hand
a flask of water from a well that should not exist
a thread from the warden's own clothing
a coin pressed into a corpse's palm before burial
the warden's name spoken in their dead language
a fragment of mirror that has reflected the warden once
a seal that no inquisitor will admit to having seen
a piece of bone marked with the warden's birth-sign
a key that locks instead of unlocks
a confession the warden never made
antechamber_themes: A warden's lobby of polished black stone
A sallyport stinking of rust and prayer
A cold vestibule lined with bound chronicles
A bell-room where the rope ends in a hand
An iron foreroom, walls scored by claws from inside
A waiting-room of polished black wood and the smell of old smoke
A vestibule of cracked glass where every step echoes twice
A small chapel with the prayer-rail worn down to bone
A clerk's office stacked with ledgers, no clerk visible
A confessional with two seats and one occupant
A cold-room hung with curing meats nobody has eaten
A hallway whose far end is lit by no visible source
A balcony overlooking the climax-chamber, with no way down
A library with one shelf taken bodily off the wall
